_comment: sovereign-os Weaver dashboard (R496, master spec § 21). Visualizes the operator-§21 Atomic State Transition Protocol — the lockless-loopback ZFS-layer atomic-rename primitive that commits state-fabric files (IDENTITY/SOUL/AGENTS/CLAUDE) without filesystem lag or concurrent-write collisions. Surfaces atomic-write counts per file × result, bytes committed, and freshness gauge per file. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Closes the weaver dashboard:FUTURE waiver and registers weaver as a first-class MODULE_COVERAGE entry (3 shipped surfaces: core / cli / dashboard).

panels.title: atomic writes (total)
panels.description: Sum-since-start across all 4 state-fabric files × all results.

panels.title: state files observed
panels.description: Distinct file labels seen. Operator-§7.1 ladder is fixed at 4: IDENTITY.md / SOUL.md / AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md.

panels.title: freshest commit (age, s)
panels.description: Operator-§21 freshness signal — staleness of the MOST RECENT atomic write across all 4 state files. Red >24h, yellow >1h.

panels.title: atomic write rate per file (5m)
panels.description: Per-state-file write rate. IDENTITY.md / SOUL.md / AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md.

panels.title: atomic write rate per result (5m)
panels.description: Per-result rate. Result vocabulary: success / dry-run / (fail emits as exception — increments aren't recorded since the primitive raises before _emit_metric).

panels.title: cumulative writes per file
panels.description: Sum-since-start per file × result. Operator-§21 lifetime distribution across the 4-file state fabric.

panels.title: bytes committed per file
panels.description: Operator-§21 payload size at commit time. The 4K-aligned padding for O_DIRECT means values are quantized to BLOCK=4096 multiples on the real I/O path.

panels.title: state fabric snapshot (file × last-commit age)
panels.description: Per-file freshness. Operator-discoverable: 'when was each state file last atomically committed?' Sentinel of the entire master spec § 21 protocol.

panels.title: weaver §21 verbatim + Atomic State Transition Protocol

options.content:
**Master spec § 21 verbatim** (The Weaver Execution, sacrosanct): > "To ensure that state adjustments across CLAUDE.md, SOUL.md, and IDENTITY.md happen without filesystem lag or concurrent write collisions, The Weaver executes a strict, lockless loopback write sequence on the ZFS layer." **Operator §7.1 state fabric (4 files, sacrosanct ladder):** 1. `IDENTITY.md` — who-I-am declarations (operator-asserted invariants) 2. `SOUL.md` — values, judgement priors, refused anti-patterns 3. `AGENTS.md` — multi-agent topology, delegation contracts 4. `CLAUDE.md` — session-bound working memory + handoff log All 4 live under `WEAVER_CONTEXT_DIR` (default `/mnt/vault/context`). **Atomic write protocol (master spec § 21.1 verbatim load-bearing primitives):** 1. `O_DIRECT` — bypass volatile OS page caches (NVMe direct path) 2. `O_SYNC` — synchronous write guarantees physical block commit 3. `O_TRUNC` — old content is fully replaced (no partial-overlay state) 4. 4K-aligned memory buffer — NVMe physical block alignment via `mmap(-1, len(payload))` 5. `os.rename(tmp_path, context_path)` — POSIX-atomic rename, the master spec § 21.1 load-bearing guarantee. NO READER EVER SEES PARTIAL STATE. The `O_DIRECT` path falls back to `O_SYNC`-only when the filesystem rejects direct I/O (tmpfs / overlayfs in containers don't support `O_DIRECT`). The atomicity guarantee is preserved by `O_SYNC + atomic rename` regardless. **ZFS layer prerequisites (master spec § 7.2 — caller's job):** - `tank/context` dataset with `sync=always` (forces synchronous semantics ALL THE WAY DOWN to the ZIL) - `recordsize=16k` recommended (accepts 4K-aligned payloads cleanly) - `compression=zstd` (markdown compresses well; no atomicity cost) **Layer-B metric contract (R496-dashboarded):** 1. Metric: `sovereign_os_weaver_atomic_write_total{file,result}` (counter; result ∈ {success, dry-run}) 2. Metric: `sovereign_os_weaver_atomic_write_bytes{file}` (gauge; last-commit payload size, 4K-padded) 3. Metric: `sovereign_os_weaver_atomic_write_last_timestamp{file}` (gauge; freshness) **CLI:** ``` scripts/weaver/atomic-state.py write --from-stdin # commit from stdin scripts/weaver/atomic-state.py write --from-file

# commit from file scripts/weaver/atomic-state.py read # read current state scripts/weaver/atomic-state.py list # enumerate the 4 state files ``` `` MUST be one of `IDENTITY.md / SOUL.md / AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md` (master spec § 7.1). **Source:** master spec § 21 shipped the Atomic State Transition Protocol blueprint; R152-R155 shipped the Python implementation + Layer-B metrics; R496 ships THIS dashboard + registers `weaver` as a first-class MODULE_COVERAGE entry (3 surfaces: core / cli / dashboard). **Standing operator rule (sacrosanct):** > "We do not minimize anything."

title: sovereign-os weaver
